mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding economic output and corporate earnings, which directly supports higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 on INDPRO over any 2-year window, or positive INDPRO leads to negative returns in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, it signals severe labor market weakness that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 5% yields positive forward returns in >50% of triggered months, or the signal has no significant predictive power (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts amplifies negative demand shocks, as consumption and construction contraction reinforce a downward feedback loop, predicting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If the combined indicator (e.g., standardized sum) does not predict negative returns with Rank IC <= -0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, a declining federal funds rate reduces discount rates and stimulates economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:fed cut rates to near zero in late 2008
failure_condition: If in months where FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month moving average (easing), forward returns are not significantly higher than in non-easing months (permutation test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5%, it indicates tight monetary policy that contracts credit and economic activity, predicting negative short-term equity returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:high rates historically precede market drawdowns
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 5% leads to positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the hit rate is indistinguishable from random (binomial test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A combined decline in payroll employment and industrial production signals a deep recession, as labor income loss and output contraction mutually reinforce, predicting negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (product of standardized PAYEMS and INDPRO changes) does not predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC <= -0.04 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, rising real GDP signals sustainable expansion and improving fundamentals, leading to positive long-term equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP turned positive in Q3 2009 after recession
failure_condition: If in quarters with positive GDP growth after a recession, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently rising consumer prices compress real earnings growth and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of inflation changes is consistently above -0.02 over a 3-year window, or if positive inflation surprises lead to positive returns in >50% of cases.
